summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #758 where the Matrix sync loop permanently dies on transient network errors instead of retrying.
summary.outcome: Implemented an exponential backoff retry loop (5s–300s) with backoff reset on healthy connections (60s threshold), committed, pushed, and opened a PR.

repo: Matrix sync is handled in crates/matrix/src/client.rs via a tokio::spawn block using a CancellationToken for clean shutdown.
Initial sync (sync_once) is intentionally allowed to fail hard at startup — only the long-running loop needed retry logic.
The project uses tracing macros (info!, warn!, error!) with structured fields like account_id = %account_id_for_sync.

code.finding: sync_once_and_spawn_loop spawns a tokio task with tokio::select! between client.sync() and a CancellationToken. Previously had no retry; now wraps in a loop with exponential backoff and a 60s healthy-connection threshold for backoff reset.
tracing import needed `error` added alongside existing info, instrument, warn.

workflow: Rust formatting check (cargo fmt --check) was run before committing and passed cleanly.
The worktree path follows the pattern /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/<branch-name>/.

friction: The initial plan omitted backoff reset on healthy reconnection — required a correction from the user before implementation.
open_items: No counter or give-up limit on retries — indefinite retry is intentional but may warrant an alerting/observability mechanism if the loop stays in backoff for extended periods.
The 60s HEALTHY_THRESHOLD for backoff reset is a heuristic; may need tuning based on observed Matrix server behavior in production.
